$comment: Mobile Progress Step Bar Component Tokens
$description: Component tokens for Mobile Progress Step Bar — a small pill-shaped colored segment used as both a standalone status indicator and as the connector bar between step dots in a multi-step stepper. Supports 6 states: Upcoming (default gray), Completed (light blue), Current (brand blue with matching border), Error (red-light), Warning (yellow-light), and Disabled (white with gray border). Figma Reference: Node ID 364-4772

$component.name: Progress Step Bar
$component.description: A pill-shaped segment that communicates the state of a step connector or step status in a stepper flow. Used in two contexts: (1) as a standalone status chip (8px × 16px) for component documentation, and (2) as a flex connector bar (4px height) between step indicator dots inside the Single Step and Progress Step components. In the connector role, width stretches flex-1 horizontally or is fixed at 35.5px (then rotated -90°) in vertical orientation.
$component.structure: A single div with pill border-radius, background color, and optional border for Current and Disabled states. No internal children.

state.Completed: Light blue (primary/300) — indicates a finished step
state.Current: Brand blue (primary/600) — active step connector; same color used for both background and border
state.Error: Light red (error/300) — step failed or has an error
state.Warning: Light yellow (warning/300) — step has a warning condition
state.Disabled: White background with gray/300 border — step is not interactive

usage.contexts: Used as sub-component inside Single Step (connector bar between dots) and Progress Step (full stepper). Also displayed standalone for state documentation.
usage.features: 6 semantic states, pill shape, dual-role sizing (standalone 8×16px / connector 4px × flexible)

standalone.$comment: Dimensions for the standalone component as shown in the component sheet

height.description: Mobile progress step bar standalone height - 8px (reference component sheet size)

width.description: Mobile progress step bar standalone width - 16px (reference component sheet size)

connector.$comment: Dimensions when used as the horizontal or vertical connector bar between step dots inside Single Step / Progress Step

height.description: Mobile progress step bar connector height - 4px (used when rendering the bar between step dots; thinner than standalone)

vertical.description: Mobile progress step bar connector fixed width for vertical orientation - 35.5px (bar is rendered at this width then rotated -90° to create the vertical connector between step dots)

border-radius.description: Mobile progress step bar border radius - fully circular pill (rounded-[64px] in Figma)

width.description: Mobile progress step bar border width - 1px (only applied in Current and Disabled states)

color.$comment: Background and optional border colors keyed by state variant

upcoming.description: Mobile progress step bar color for Upcoming (default) state - gray/200 - #eaecf0 (unfilled/pending step connector)

completed.description: Mobile progress step bar color for Completed state - primary/blue/300 - #84adff (finished step connector, lighter blue than active)

background.description: Mobile progress step bar background for Current state - primary/blue/600 - #155eef

border.description: Mobile progress step bar border for Current state - primary/blue/600 - #155eef (same as background, creates a solid bordered pill effect)

error.description: Mobile progress step bar color for Error state - error/300 - #fda29b (light red indicating a failed step connector)

warning.description: Mobile progress step bar color for Warning state - warning/300 - #fec84b (light yellow indicating a step with a warning)

background.description: Mobile progress step bar background for Disabled state - white (step connector is inactive/non-interactive)

border.description: Mobile progress step bar border for Disabled state - gray/300 - #d0d5dd
